channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: The roof collapses at a Dominican Republic nightclub, killing at least 98 people
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The roof of an iconic nightclub in the Dominican capital collapsed early Tuesday during a merengue concert attended by politicians, athletes and others, leaving at least 98 people dead and 160 injured, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social worker imprisoned nearly 4 years for mediation attempts at Hong Kong protests
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A Hong Kong social worker was sentenced to three years and nine months in prison Wednesday on a charge of riot for her role in the 2019 anti-government movement, in a case that signals the authorities' stern approach to protest-related acts.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: King Charles III’s fairy tale isn’t the one we remember as marriage to Camilla reaches 20-year mark
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla met more than 50 years ago but their romance had to survive meddling families, marriages to other people and no small amount of public ridicule before they finally married.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korean leader's sister denounces denuclearization calls, saying nuclear status is permanent
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un on Wednesday mocked Washington and its Asian allies for what she called their “daydream” of denuclearizing the North, insisting that the country will never give up its nuclear weapons program.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says 2 Chinese were caught fighting alongside Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian military has captured two Chinese men fighting alongside the Russian army in the eastern Donetsk region and has information that “significantly more” are with Russian forces, Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US defense secretary says China is a threat to Panama Canal, spurring a fiery response from Beijing
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday the Panama Canal faces ongoing threats from China but that together the United States and Panama will keep it secure.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The IMF reaches a deal with troubled Argentina on a $20 billion bailout
item.description: The International Monetary Fund has reached a preliminary agreement with Argentina on a $20 billion bailout package, providing a reprieve to President Javier Milei as he seeks to overturn the country’s old economic order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hegseth: US, Panama will 'take back' canal from Chinese influence
item.description: The United States and Panama will work together to take back the Panama Canal from “China’s influence,” Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday while standing in front of the vital shipping lane. Hegseth, who was in Panama to meet with its government officials, spoke from a new U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Iran's foreign minister says he will have indirect talks with US envoy over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran 's foreign minister said Tuesday he'll meet with U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff in Oman for the first negotiations under the Trump administration seeking to halt Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program as tensions remain high in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and AMIR VAHDAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indigenous march in Brazil to demand more land be set aside for their stewardship
item.description: BRASILIA, Brazil (AP) — With the first U.N. climate talks in the Amazon approaching, thousands of Indigenous people marched Tuesday in Brazil’s capital, demanding the state guarantee and expand their rights to traditional lands as part of the solution to the world’s climate crisis.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House insists Trump won't budge on 104 percent China tariff
item.description: The White House on Tuesday insisted that President Trump won’t back down on his threat to impose a more than 100 percent tariff rate on China, significantly escalating the trade war between the two nations hours before the deadline approaches.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: State Department reviewing 'incident' with Rubio's security in Brussels
item.description: The State Department said it is reviewing an “incident” with Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s security detail during last week’s trip to Europe. A Diplomatic Security Service (DSS) shift supervisor reportedly was arrested and released following an altercation with local police.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 2 Chinese men captured in Ukraine, Zelenskyy claims
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called for action in the U.S. and Europe after claiming to capture two Chinese nationals fighting in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boris Johnson pecked by ostrich
item.description: Former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson had a fowl encounter with an ostrich during a sight-seeing trip in Texas, with video showing him getting nipped by the bird while his son laughed in his lap.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: Top Democrat announces measure to curb Trump on tariffs
item.description: Sen. Ron Wyden (Ore.), the top Democrat on the Senate Finance Committee, announced Tuesday he’s introducing a resolution to thwart President Trump’s latest tariffs actions.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, The Hill

item.title: An Israeli strike hit near a charity kitchen in Gaza as Palestinians gathered for food
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on Monday hit next to a charity kitchen where Palestinians crowded to receive cooked meals as food supplies dwindle under Israel's monthlong blockade of the Gaza Strip, one of a string of attacks in the territory that killed more than 30 people, mos
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US admiral at NATO fired in expanding national security purge
item.description: U.S. Navy Vice Admiral Shoshana Chatfield is one of only a handful of female Navy three-star officers.
item.dc:creator: Phil Stewart, Idrees Ali, Lili Bayer / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: UN mission details deadly attacks in Haiti's capital and questions government's delayed response
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — More than 260 people were killed during recent gang attacks on two communities in Haiti’s capital, according to a report on Monday by the U.N. political mission in Haiti that questioned the delay in the response by authorities.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine will send a team to the US next week for talks on a new draft mineral deal
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine will send a team to Washington next week to begin negotiations on a new draft of a deal that would give the U.S. access to Ukraine’s valuable mineral resources, Economy Minister Yuliia Svyrydenko told The Associated Press. “The new draft agreement from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Belgium charges 8 people in European Union Parliament bribery probe
item.description: Following a probe into suspected bribery at the European Parliament, eight people have been charged with money laundering, corruption and participation in a criminal organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN official reappointed despite accusations of antisemitism
item.description: Allegations of antisemitism and the condemnation of several nations did not stop Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese from securing another three-year term in her position.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New ecosystem found after Chicago-sized chunk of Antarctica breaks off
item.description: They found animal and marine life as deep as nearly 4,300 feet. The species in the newly discovered ecosystem include crustaceans, sponges, corals and octopus.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Sick sea lions, other mammals stranded on beaches in Baja, Southern California
item.description: Officials are reporting an unusually high number of beached animals due "domoic acid or phytotoxin" in the water.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: US will know in 'matter of weeks' if Russia is serious about peace or using 'delay tactic': Rubio
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the U.S. will know within weeks whether Russia is serious about a ceasefire with Ukraine or if it's time to rethink U.S. policy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials say a Russian strike in central Ukraine kills 14 people, including six children
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian ballistic missile strike Friday on a central Ukrainian city killed at least 14 people, including six children, Ukrainian officials said, as U.S. and European leaders pressed Russia to accept a ceasefire in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luxury cruise ship caught in 40-foot waves while traveling through Drake Passage
item.description: Passengers on Quark Expeditions' Ocean Explorer ship got rocked with waves up to 40 feet on a recent cruise, rocking those onboard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strike on a school in Gaza kills at least 27 people, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 100 Palestinians across the Gaza Strip on Thursday, including 27 or more sheltering at a school, according to Palestinian medical authorities, in a stepped-up offensive that Israel’s military said is intended to pressure Hamas and e
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White House defends not including Russia, North Korea on tariffs
item.description: The official said that President Trump has “recently threatened to impose strong sanctions on Russia” to further explain leaving out Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli leader Netanyahu will visit Hungary, defying an international arrest warrant
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is scheduled to arrive in Hungary on Wednesday to meet with its nationalist prime minister despite an international arrest warrant for the Israeli leader over the war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation
